sprint.title: Email Templates v1: React Email (Branded, Cross-Client, Auth + Billing)
sprint.goal: Create beautiful, branded transactional email templates using React Email that render consistently across major email clients. Include Memorioso logo/theme, dark-safe styling, accessible buttons, and plain-text fallbacks. Ship templates needed for Auth + Billing flows.
definitionOfDone: React Email set up in repo with preview/dev workflow
Core branded layout (header/logo, container, typography, footer) implemented as reusable components
Auth templates created: Verify Email, Login Code/Magic Link, Reset Password, Password Changed (optional)
Billing templates created: Subscription Active, Payment Failed, Invoice/Receipt (optional if using Stripe portal)
All templates render well in common clients per React Email compatibility tooling
Templates support variables (name, code, link, expiry, support link) and have plain-text fallbacks
Emails are safe (no external hotlinked images required; logo served reliably; links validated)
Docs added: how to add/edit templates, how to preview/test, and brand tokens

stackAssumptions.sender: Provider-agnostic (can use Resend/SES later); templates generate HTML

sourceNotes.reactEmailIntro: React Email provides components and a tooling workflow for building email-client friendly HTML, with a compatibility checker.

backlog.title: Set up React Email in the repo (dev preview + build output HTML)

notes: React Email is designed for incremental adoption and quick setup.
Templates can be rendered to HTML for any sending provider.

acceptanceTests: Local preview server works and shows sample template

backlog.title: Create Memorioso email design system (tokens + reusable layout components)

logo: Include Memorioso logo via Img component
Prefer hosting logo from your own domain/static asset (stable)
constraints: Use email-safe layout primitives (tables/sections via React Email components)
Avoid unsupported CSS; favor inline-safe styles

acceptanceTests: Shell renders consistently with header/footer and correct spacing
Logo displays with alt text and no layout shift

backlog.title: Auth template set v1 (Verify Email, Login Code/Magic Link, Reset Password)

copyRules: Explain what to do in one line
Include fallback text if button doesn't work (plain URL)
Include security line: 'If you didn’t request this, ignore this email.'
constraints: Provide plain-text alternative output for each email
Links must be validated by linter tooling where possible

acceptanceTests: Each template renders with provided variables
Plain-text fallback exists and matches intent

backlog.title: Billing template set v1 (Subscription Active, Payment Failed, Invoice/Receipt optional)

templates.subject: Payment failed — action needed for Memorioso

optional: invoice_receipt (if you want a branded wrapper over Stripe invoice links)
constraints: Do not include sensitive billing details in email body; link to portal/invoice

acceptanceTests: Templates render correctly with portal link
Copy is clear and not alarming

backlog.title: Cross-client compatibility pass (React Email compatibility checker + CSS hardening)

checks: Run React Email compatibility tooling and fix unsupported styles
Avoid complex CSS (position:fixed, advanced gradients, webfonts-only assumptions)
Ensure buttons render as bulletproof buttons (table-based) via components
constraints: Prefer simple layouts: Container -> Sections -> Text -> Button

acceptanceTests: Templates pass compatibility checks with minimal warnings

backlog.title: Variable contract + validation (fail fast if variables missing)

contracts: Define TypeScript types per template variables
Add runtime validation (zod) for required vars
constraints: Do not send partially rendered emails
Log only templateId + missing var names (no PII)

acceptanceTests: Missing vars are caught before sending
Send layer returns stable error codes

backlog.title: Provider integration layer (render React Email -> HTML -> send) with a pluggable sender

design: Keep provider behind interface so you can switch Resend/SES later
constraints: Do not block auth flows if email provider temporarily fails; surface a safe message
Rate limit auth emails per user/email
notes: React Email templates can be sent via any provider.

acceptanceTests: Sender interface can be swapped without changing templates

backlog.title: Preview gallery + snapshot testing for templates (regression protection)

gallery: Create a preview index page in React Email dev server for all templates
tests: Snapshot HTML output for each template (or key sections) to detect accidental layout changes

acceptanceTests: Templates can be previewed quickly by non-dev team members

backlog.title: Final polish: Memorioso theme, accessibility, and deliverability hygiene

constraints: No tracking pixels in v1 (keep it simple)

acceptanceTests: Emails look premium and readable on mobile
Plain-text version is clean and usable

notes: Use React Email’s compatibility checker to avoid CSS that breaks in Outlook/Gmail.
Keep templates provider-agnostic: render to HTML/text, then send with whichever provider you choose.
